zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Alice Cotton于1446年出生在英国坎布利其爾时，她的父亲Sir William Cotton26岁，母亲Alice Anne Abbott38岁。她和Thomas Heigham至少有5个儿子和7个女儿。

nameMeanings.name: Cotton
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: habitational name from any of numerous places called from Old English (æt) cotum (dative plural of cot) ‘at the cottages or huts’ (or sometimes possibly from a Middle English plural, coten). Examples include Coton (Cambridgeshire, Northamptonshire, Staffordshire), Cottam (East Yorkshire, Lancashire, Nottinghamshire), and Cotham (Nottinghamshire). Particularly common in Staffordshire.
French: from a pet form of the personal name Nicolas (see Nicholas ), derived via Nicot, or of the personal name Jacques , derived via Jacot.
History: John Cotton (1584–1652) was a noted Puritan preacher, who landed at Boston, MA, from London in 1633 and became leader of the Congregationalists in America.

variants.name: Cotten
meanings: Altered form of German Kotten, a habitational name from any of several places so named in Rhineland, Westphalia, Silesia, etc., or perhaps an American shortened form of composite German surnames such as Kottenhagen, Kottenhoff, Kottenkamp (see Koth ).

variants.name: Nicholas
meanings: English (southwestern England and south Wales) and Dutch: from the personal name Nicholas (from Latin Nicolaus, from Greek Nikolaos, from nikān ‘to conquer’ + laos ‘people’). Forms with -ch- are due to hypercorrection (compare Anthony ). The name in various vernacular forms was popular among Christians throughout Europe in the Middle Ages, largely as a result of the fame of a 4th-century Lycian bishop, about whom a large number of legends grew up, and who was venerated in the Orthodox Church as well as the Catholic. In North America, the English form of the surname has absorbed cognates from other languages and also their patronymics and other derivatives, e.g. Croatian and Serbian Nikolić (see Nikolic ), Greek Papanikolaou ‘(son of) Nicholas the priest’ and Nikolopoulos . Compare Nickolas .

History: The colonial official and revolutionary patriot Robert Carter Nicholas was from a prominent VA family on both sides. His father was a British navy surgeon who emigrated c. 1700 from Lancashire, England, to Williamsburg, VA.

variants.name: Cotham
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of   Cotton  .

variants.name: Cottam
meanings: English (Midlands and northern England): variant of   Cotton  .

nameMeanings.name: Alice
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a variant of Adelaide , representing an Old French spelling of a reduced form of Germanic Adalheidis. Alice and Adelaide were already regarded as distinct names in English during the medieval period. Alice enjoyed a surge of popularity in the 19th century and periods of favour ever since. It was the name of the central character of Lewis Carroll's Alice's Adventures in Wonderland ( 1865 ) and Through the Looking Glass ( 1872 ), who was based on his child friend Alice Liddell , daughter of the Dean of Christ Church, Oxford.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

en.lifeSummary: When Alice Cotton was born in 1446, in Cambridgeshire, England, her father, Sir William Cotton, was 26 and her mother, Alice Anne Abbott, was 38. She had at least 5 sons and 7 daughters with Thomas Heigham.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Alice Cotton est née en 1446, à Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, son père, Sir William Cotton, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Alice Anne Abbott, avait 38 ans. Elle a eu au moins 5 fils et 7 filles avec Thomas Heigham.

de.lifeSummary: Als Alice Cotton 1446 in Cambridgeshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir William Cotton 26 und ihre Mutter Alice Anne Abbott 38. Sie hatte mit Thomas Heigham mindestens 5 Söhne und 7 Töchter.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Cotton è nata il nel 1446 nel Cambridgeshire, England, sua padre, Sir William Cotton, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Alice Anne Abbott, aveva 38 anni. Lei avuto almeno 5 figli e 7 figlie con Thomas Heigham.

ja.lifeSummary: Alice Cottonが1446年にイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir William Cottonは26歳，母Alice Anne Abbottは38歳であった。彼女はThomas Heighamとの間に少なくとも5人の息子と7人の娘をもうけた。

ko.lifeSummary: Alice Cotton(이)가 1446년 영국 캄브리지실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir William Cotton(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Alice Anne Abbott(은)는 38세였다. 그녀는 Thomas Heigham(와)과 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 일곱 명의 딸을 두었다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Alice Cotton nasceu em 1446, em Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Sir William Cotton, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Alice Anne Abbott, tinha 38 anos. Ela teve pelo menos 5 filhos e 7 filhas com Thomas Heigham.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Alice Cotton родилась в 1446 г. в местности камбриджешир, Англия, её отцу по имени Sir William Cotton было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Alice Anne Abbott, 38. У неё было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 7 дочерей от Thomas Heigham.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Alice Cotton nació en 1446, en Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Sir William Cotton, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Alice Anne Abbott, tenía 38 años. Tuvo por lo menos 5 hijos y 7 hijas con Thomas Heigham.
